title: howdynox
tagline: A static mirror of the RPLY site with exactly one job: hijack every download button.

title: howdynox-app
tagline: The real heynox.com source, redeployed on a second domain so the campaign never touches prod.

title: heynox-proto
tagline: "Everyone is screaming at you. One calm inbox." — landing-page prototypes for the NOX pitch.

title: heynox-ad
tagline: A 9-second Revolut-style TikTok ad for RPLY, rendered for $0 — down to the synthesized soundtrack.

title: heynox-higgsfield
tagline: The tunnel ad again, but AI-video: WebGL frames in, Higgsfield cinematics out.

title: nox-explode
tagline: Click anywhere, detonate an explosion in slow motion. There's a slider for how slow.

title: nox-logo-explode
tagline: Two more ways to destroy the NOX logo: detonate, or shatter it like glass.

title: nox-domain-sites
tagline: 22 domains, 22 AI-written brand voices, one script to attach them all to Vercel.

title: nox-redirect
tagline: A tiny 302 with a memory — token-checked download links that log every click.

title: RPLY Console
tagline: The campaign war room: influencer finder, burn-and-replace mail pool, and a go-live runbook.

title: RPLY Contacts
tagline: An iPhone app that cleans your address book — first names, monogram avatars, merged dupes. All on-device.

title: RPLY Phone
tagline: RPLY on iPhone via the only loophole iOS allows: a keyboard that reads your clipboard.

title: rply-launch
tagline: The Product Hunt + Show HN launch package for RPLY, written and waiting on founder sign-off.

title: HopperLeads: heynox edition
tagline: The homeowner-leads desktop app, rebadged to drip 116 scored RPLY influencer prospects.

title: heynox-referral
tagline: A one-file NOX fan landing page with a slot for your referral code. Swap one constant, deploy anywhere.

title: Crew Kit
tagline: The madebyangus tiny construction crew as React components: fifteen workers, a crane, an hourglass.
